job_listing.company_values: Scalability, ethical AI, and high-performance computing.
job_listing.job_description: We need an engineer to deploy computer vision models at scale. Experience with PyTorch and MLOps tools like Kubeflow is required.

candidate_resume.summary: Postdoctoral researcher specializing in Transformer architectures and model accuracy optimization.

experience.responsibilities: Published 4 papers on model distillation. Achieved SOTA results on ImageNet benchmarks.

suggested_interviewer_questions: Your research background is impressive, but how would you handle a model that performs well in the lab but experiences significant drift in production?
Can you explain your experience using Docker to containerize an ML model for a Kubernetes cluster?

sample_candidate_responses.response: I would implement a monitoring pipeline to track data distribution shifts. If drift is detected, I’d trigger an automated re-training loop with the latest ground-truth data to ensure the model stays performant.
While most of my work has been in local environments, I understand that Docker ensures the model has the exact CUDA and library dependencies needed to run consistently across different cloud nodes.

must_flag: If the interviewer fails to probe the candidate on their lack of professional Kubernetes/DevOps experience.

guardrail_checks.topic_lock: Professional interview and resume prep only.
